content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN positive integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,‚Ä¶,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã where NNN is **odd**. You will perform the following operation repeatedly until only one element remains:


Choose any three consecutive elements in the array, say Ai,Ai+1,A_i, A_{i+1},Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã, and Ai+2A_{i+2}Ai+2‚Äã for some valid index iii. Remove these three elements and replace them in the same position with a single integer equal to max‚Å°(Ai+1,Ai+1,Ai+2+1)max‚Å°(A_i + 1, A_{i+1}, A_{i+2} + 1)max‚Å°(Ai‚Äã+1,Ai+1‚Äã,Ai+2‚Äã+1).
Your task is to determine the maximum possible value of the final remaining element if you perform the operations in an optimal order.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1,A_2,...,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible value of the final remaining element after performing the operations optimally.
constraints: It is guaranteed that NNN is odd.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Only one element exists, so the final remaining value is 6.
Test Case 2: Apply the operation on all three elements: max(2+1,2,2+1)=3max(2+1, 2, 2+1) = 3max(2+1,2,2+1)=3. Final remaining value = 3.
Test Case 3: Apply the operation on all three elements: max(1+1,2,1+1)=2max(1+1, 2, 1+1) = 2max(1+1,2,1+1)=2. Final remaining value = 2.
